title: Wealth Rules — The $1M Exercise & Setting Goals
learningObjectives: Understand that $1M is achievable with discipline and time
Set SMART financial goals across short, medium, and long term
keyPrinciples: A million dollars is 1000 x $1,000 — earned in steps
Goals without deadlines are just wishes
Your financial identity shapes every choice
storyPrompts: The math teacher writes on the board: '$1,000,000.' The class laughs. Then she breaks it down: 30 years, $10/week, 10% average return. '$1,003,402,' she finishes. The laughter stops. {name} stares at the number and wonders if they've been thinking too small.

{name} writes out three financial goals: one for next month, one for next year, one for next decade. 'Goals are just decisions made in advance,' a mentor says. 'You're deciding who you want to be before the moment arrives.'

The {item} in the store window still glitters. But {name} sees it differently now. Not as a thing to want, but as a choice — against {amount} invested, against a goal, against the version of themselves they're building. The math has changed everything.

decisionCards.scenario: Someone asks: 'Do you think you'll ever be a millionaire?' What do you say?
decisionCards.context: Your belief shapes your behavior. Your behavior shapes your bank account.

choices.label: 'I don't know, but I'm building toward it.'

decisionCards.scenario: You have enough money to hit an early goal 2 months ahead of schedule.
decisionCards.context: Beating your own timeline is the best feeling.

choices.label: Set the next, bigger goal immediately
Give back: donate some to someone starting out

decisionCards.scenario: Write your Owner's Manifesto. What's your #1 money rule?
decisionCards.context: Identity precedes behavior. Name your rule.

bossChallengeTemplates.scenario: FINAL BOSS: Your younger sibling asks you how to become rich. What do you say?
bossChallengeTemplates.context: Teaching cements learning. What you know, you can share.

choices.label: 'Pay yourself first, use 3 buckets, invest early.'
'Let me show you the $1M math.'
choices.description: Give them the compound interest gift.

bossChallengeTemplates.scenario: FINAL BOSS: You have $1,000, 30 years, and a choice of three paths.
bossChallengeTemplates.context: Put the whole course to work.

choices.label: Spend it — it's a small amount anyway
choices.description: Small amounts compound to large amounts.

choices.label: Invest it and forget it for 30 years
Invest it AND add $50/month forever
choices.description: The wealth formula: lump sum + consistent contribution.

bossChallengeTemplates.scenario: FINAL BOSS: You've finished the Owner's Path. What kind of owner will you be?
bossChallengeTemplates.context: The answer defines the next chapter.

choices.label: The Saver: safety, security, slow and steady
The Investor: compound and grow, think in decades
The Builder: create businesses, employ others, multiply value

forecasts: You've completed the Owner's Path. The real game starts now — in real life.
Your traits reflect your journey. Discipline and Ownership are your strongest.
The $1M path is possible. The math is real. The choice is yours.
Share your seed with someone you want to challenge. The family league awaits.
